title: 7 Common Leash Training Mistakes (2026) – And How to Fix Them
seoTitle: Common Leash Training Mistakes – How to Fix Them | GetPawsy
seoDescription: 7 leash training mistakes that make pulling worse. Wrong equipment, inconsistency, and timing errors explained with fixes.
h1Override: 7 Common Leash Training Mistakes – And How to Fix Them
excerpt: Seven mistakes that sabotage leash training and their simple fixes. Equipment errors, timing issues, and inconsistency patterns.
quickAnswer: The top 3 leash training mistakes: (1) Using a retractable leash (teaches pulling), (2) Allowing pulling 'sometimes' (intermittent reinforcement makes it permanent), (3) Using a back-clip harness on a puller (encourages sled-dog mechanics).

faq.question: Is it too late to fix leash pulling?
faq.answer: Never. Dogs of any age can learn loose-leash walking. Older dogs with established habits take 3–4 weeks instead of 1–2, but the same positive reinforcement techniques work regardless of age.

sections.heading: The 7 Mistakes
sections.content:
**1. Retractable Leash:** Teaches dogs that pulling extends their range. Use a fixed 6-foot leash only.

**2. Back-Clip Harness on a Puller:** Encourages pulling through sled-dog mechanics. Switch to front-clip. Browse [no-pull harnesses →](/collections/dog-leash-control).

**3. Inconsistency:** If pulling works sometimes (rushing to the park), it's reinforced permanently. Be 100% consistent.

**4. Pulling Back:** Yanking the leash teaches your dog to pull harder (opposition reflex). Stop moving instead.

**5. No Rewards:** Loose-leash walking is boring for dogs. Make it worth their while with high-value treats.

**6. Too Long Sessions:** 15–20 minutes max for training walks. Separate training from exercise.

**7. Starting Outdoors:** Build foundation skills indoors first where distractions are minimal.

For the correct protocol, read our [Leash Training Step-by-Step Guide →](/guides/leash-training-dog-step-by-step).
